introduction: This spec defines `afc`, the command-line client for af-hub, covering the full OAuth login flow and API key management subcommands. Note: requirements referencing Cobra and net/http APIs are based on unverified PRD assumptions and should be cross-checked against the installed library versions before implementation.

glossary.--api-key: CLI flag that supplies an API key for authentication, overrides AF_HUB_API_KEY
glossary.--hub-url: CLI flag that specifies the hub server base URL, overrides AF_HUB_URL
glossary.--provider: CLI flag on afc login specifying the OAuth provider name (defaults to github)
glossary.AF_HUB_API_KEY: Environment variable supplying an API key for authentication on key management commands
glossary.AF_HUB_URL: Environment variable specifying the hub server base URL
glossary.Cobra: The Go CLI framework (github.com/spf13/cobra) used to build the afc binary
glossary.GET /api/v1/auth/providers: Hub endpoint that returns the list of available OAuth providers
glossary.POST /api/v1/auth/callback: Hub endpoint that accepts the OAuth authorization code and returns a user object
glossary.af-hub: The hub server that owns all state and exposes the API consumed by the CLI
glossary.afc: The CLI binary name for the af-hub command-line client
glossary.afc keys create: Subcommand that creates a new API key scoped to a workspace
glossary.afc keys list: Subcommand that lists all API keys for the authenticated user
glossary.afc keys refresh: Subcommand that generates a new secret for an existing API key
glossary.afc keys revoke: Subcommand that permanently revokes an existing API key
glossary.afc login: Subcommand that runs the OAuth authorization code flow
glossary.authorization code flow: OAuth 2.0 authorization code grant: redirect user to provider, receive code at callback, exchange code for tokens on the server
glossary.callback server: A short-lived local HTTP server started by afc to receive the OAuth redirect containing the authorization code
glossary.docs/cli.md: The CLI reference documentation file shipped with the project
glossary.error envelope: Standard JSON error response shape returned by the hub on HTTP errors, containing a machine-readable error and human-readable message
glossary.expires_at: Timestamp field on a key object indicating when the key expires
glossary.key_id: Unique identifier for an API key resource on the hub
glossary.redirect_uri: The OAuth parameter specifying the local callback URL including the randomly chosen port
glossary.revoked status: Boolean field on a key object indicating whether the key has been permanently revoked
glossary.workspace_id: Unique identifier for a workspace resource on the hub
glossary.keys: CLI subcommand group for API key management.
glossary.--provider <provider>: CLI flag specifying the OAuth provider name.
glossary.--expires: CLI flag specifying key expiry in days.
glossary.--workspace <workspace-id>: CLI flag specifying the target workspace ID.
glossary.--expires 0|30|60|90: CLI flag with allowed expiry values in days.
glossary.--label <label>: CLI flag specifying a human-readable label for an API key.
glossary.--workspace: CLI flag specifying the target workspace.

user_story.goal: run a structured CLI binary called afc with clear subcommands
user_story.benefit: I can discover and use hub operations from the terminal without reading source code

acceptance_criteria.action: is built as a Go binary named `afc` using `Cobra`, with top-level subcommands `login` and `keys`
accepts `--hub-url` flag on every command, falling back to the `AF_HUB_URL` environment variable when the flag is absent
prints all JSON results to stdout and all human-readable status or error messages to stderr
exits with a non-zero exit code
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code != 0; human-readable error message on stderr
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a command fails for any reason

acceptance_criteria.action: prints usage information including available subcommands and global flags
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 0; usage text on stdout or stderr per Cobra default behavior
acceptance_criteria.trigger: invoked without a subcommand or with --help

edge_cases.action: prints an error message to stderr describing the missing configuration and exits with a non-zero exit code
edge_cases.return_contract: exit code != 0; descriptive error on stderr
edge_cases.error_condition: neither `--hub-url` flag nor `AF_HUB_URL` env var is set when a command requiring the hub URL is executed

user_story.goal: authenticate with the hub via OAuth using afc login
user_story.benefit: I can obtain credentials without manually navigating browser flows or crafting HTTP requests

acceptance_criteria.action: fetches the provider list from `GET /api/v1/auth/providers` on the hub and validates that the requested provider exists in the list
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: proceeds to step 3 on success; exits non-zero with error listing available providers if provider not found
acceptance_criteria.trigger: invoked with `--provider <provider>` (defaulting to github when omitted)

acceptance_criteria.action: starts a local HTTP `callback server` on a random available port, constructs the `redirect_uri` from the local address, and opens the provider's authorization URL in the user's default browser using os/exec with platform-appropriate commands (open on macOS, xdg-open on Linux)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: browser opens to authorization URL; local callback server is listening

acceptance_criteria.action: sends the authorization code to `POST /api/v1/auth/callback` on the hub and prints the returned user object as JSON to stdout
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: JSON user object on stdout; exit code 0
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the OAuth provider redirects back to the local `callback server` with an authorization code

acceptance_criteria.action: shuts down the local `callback server` and releases the port
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the login flow completes or fails

edge_cases.action: prints an error message to stderr that includes the attempted hub URL and connection failure details, then exits with a non-zero exit code
edge_cases.return_contract: exit code != 0; connection error details on stderr
edge_cases.error_condition: the hub is unreachable when fetching `GET /api/v1/auth/providers`

edge_cases.action: times out, shuts down the `callback server`, prints an error to stderr with a retry suggestion, and exits with a non-zero exit code
edge_cases.return_contract: exit code != 0; timeout error with retry suggestion on stderr
edge_cases.error_condition: the OAuth `callback server` does not receive a callback within 5 minutes

edge_cases.action: shuts down the `callback server`, prints the provider's error description to stderr, and exits with a non-zero exit code
edge_cases.return_contract: exit code != 0; provider error description on stderr
edge_cases.error_condition: the OAuth provider redirects to the `callback server` with an error parameter instead of an authorization code

edge_cases.action: parses the `error envelope` from the response, prints a human-readable message to stderr, and exits with a non-zero exit code
edge_cases.return_contract: exit code != 0; parsed error message on stderr
edge_cases.error_condition: the hub returns an HTTP error from `POST /api/v1/auth/callback`

edge_cases.action: shuts down the `callback server`, releases the port, and exits cleanly with a non-zero exit code
edge_cases.return_contract: exit code != 0; no orphaned listener processes or open ports
edge_cases.error_condition: the process is interrupted (SIGINT/SIGTERM) while the `callback server` is waiting for the OAuth redirect

user_story.goal: create a new API key scoped to a workspace
user_story.benefit: I can grant programmatic access to a specific workspace without using my personal credentials

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a create-key request to the hub using the `--api-key` or `AF_HUB_API_KEY` credential, defaulting expiry to 30 days when `--expires` is omitted
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: on success: full key object including plaintext secret on stdout as JSON; exit code 0

acceptance_criteria.action: prints the plaintext key secret to stdout exactly once and never logs or re-displays it

edge_cases.action: prints an error to stderr indicating the user lacks membership in the workspace and exits with a non-zero exit code
edge_cases.return_contract: exit code != 0; membership error on stderr
edge_cases.error_condition: the authenticated user is not a member of the specified workspace

edge_cases.action: prints a usage error to stderr indicating the flag is required and exits with a non-zero exit code
edge_cases.return_contract: exit code != 0; usage error on stderr

edge_cases.action: prints an error to stderr indicating that authentication credentials are required and exits with a non-zero exit code
edge_cases.return_contract: exit code != 0; missing credential error on stderr

user_story.goal: list all my API keys across workspaces
user_story.benefit: I can audit and manage programmatic access from the terminal

acceptance_criteria.action: fetches all API keys for the authenticated user from the hub and prints them to stdout as a JSON array
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: JSON array of key objects each containing key_id, label, workspace_id, expires_at, created_at, and revoked status fields; exit code 0
acceptance_criteria.trigger: invoked with a valid `--api-key` or `AF_HUB_API_KEY` credential

edge_cases.action: parses the `error envelope` and prints a human-readable message to stderr, then exits with a non-zero exit code
edge_cases.return_contract: exit code != 0; parsed error message on stderr
edge_cases.error_condition: the hub returns an HTTP error (e.g. 401 unauthorized)

user_story.goal: rotate the secret of an existing API key by key ID
user_story.benefit: I can invalidate a potentially compromised secret without revoking the key's identity and workspace association

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a refresh request to the hub for the specified `key_id` and prints the new full key object including the new plaintext secret to stdout as JSON
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: JSON key object with new plaintext secret on stdout; exit code 0
acceptance_criteria.trigger: invoked with a `key_id` argument and a valid `--api-key` or `AF_HUB_API_KEY` credential

edge_cases.action: parses the hub `error envelope` and prints a human-readable error to stderr, then exits with a non-zero exit code
edge_cases.return_contract: exit code != 0; error message on stderr
edge_cases.error_condition: the specified `key_id` does not exist or the authenticated user does not own it

edge_cases.action: prints a usage error to stderr indicating a key ID is required and exits with a non-zero exit code
edge_cases.return_contract: exit code != 0; usage error on stderr

user_story.goal: permanently revoke an API key by key ID
user_story.benefit: I can immediately cut off programmatic access associated with a specific key

acceptance_criteria.action: sends a revoke request to the hub for the specified `key_id` and prints a confirmation message to stderr
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: confirmation message on stderr; exit code 0
acceptance_criteria.trigger: invoked with a `key_id` argument and a valid `--api-key` or `AF_HUB_API_KEY` credential

edge_cases.action: parses the hub `error envelope` and prints a human-readable error to stderr, then exits with a non-zero exit code
edge_cases.return_contract: exit code != 0; error message on stderr
edge_cases.error_condition: the specified `key_id` does not exist or the authenticated user does not own it

edge_cases.action: prints a usage error to stderr indicating a key ID is required and exits with a non-zero exit code
edge_cases.return_contract: exit code != 0; usage error on stderr

user_story.goal: receive clear error messages when hub API calls fail
user_story.benefit: I can diagnose and fix issues without inspecting raw HTTP responses

acceptance_criteria.action: parses the `error envelope` from the response body and prints a human-readable message derived from the envelope to stderr
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: human-readable error message on stderr; exit code != 0
acceptance_criteria.trigger: any hub HTTP call returns a non-2xx status code

acceptance_criteria.action: falls back to printing the raw HTTP status code and response body to stderr
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP status code and raw body on stderr; exit code != 0
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the hub returns a non-2xx response with a body that cannot be parsed as the standard `error envelope`

user_story.benefit: I can learn all commands, flags, and environment variables without reading source code

acceptance_criteria.action: ships a `docs/cli.md` file that documents all commands and subcommands, all flags, all environment variables (`AF_HUB_URL`, `AF_HUB_API_KEY`), and includes at least one usage example per command
updates README.md to include a link to `docs/cli.md`

correctness_properties.title: Callback server is always shut down after login
correctness_properties.for_any: execution of `afc login` regardless of outcome (success, timeout, error, or interrupt)
correctness_properties.invariant: the local HTTP `callback server` is shut down and the port is released before the process exits

correctness_properties.title: Plaintext key secret printed exactly once
correctness_properties.for_any: successful execution of `afc keys create` or `afc keys refresh`
correctness_properties.invariant: the plaintext key secret appears on stdout exactly once and is never written to stderr, log output, or any other output stream

correctness_properties.title: JSON always goes to stdout, messages always go to stderr
correctness_properties.invariant: machine-readable JSON output is written exclusively to stdout and human-readable messages (including errors and confirmations) are written exclusively to stderr

correctness_properties.title: Non-zero exit code on any failure
correctness_properties.for_any: any command that encounters an error condition
correctness_properties.invariant: the process exits with a non-zero exit code and emits at least one error message to stderr

correctness_properties.title: Hub URL resolution precedence
correctness_properties.for_any: any command invocation where both `--hub-url` flag and `AF_HUB_URL` env var are set
correctness_properties.invariant: the value from `--hub-url` takes precedence over `AF_HUB_URL`

steps.action: runs `afc login --provider github --hub-url https://hub.example.com`
sends GET /api/v1/auth/providers to the hub and receives the provider list
validates github is in the provider list, starts local callback server on a random port, constructs redirect_uri
opens the GitHub authorization URL in the default browser via os/exec
authenticates in the browser and approves the OAuth application
redirects the browser to the local callback server with the authorization code
receives the authorization code from the redirect request
sends POST /api/v1/auth/callback with the authorization code to the hub
exchanges the code for tokens with GitHub, creates or updates the user record, and returns the user object
shuts down the callback server, prints the user object as JSON to stdout, and exits with code 0

execution_paths.title: Create API key and use it to list keys

steps.action: runs `afc keys create --workspace ws-123 --label ci-bot --expires 30 --api-key <key> --hub-url https://hub.example.com`
sends a create-key HTTP request to the hub authenticated with the provided --api-key
validates workspace membership, creates the API key, and returns the full key object including the plaintext secret
prints the full key object including plaintext secret as JSON to stdout and exits with code 0
runs `afc keys list --api-key <key> --hub-url https://hub.example.com`
sends a list-keys HTTP request to the hub authenticated with the provided --api-key
returns a list of all API keys owned by the authenticated user
prints the JSON array of key objects to stdout and exits with code 0

execution_paths.title: Revoke API key after refreshing it

steps.action: runs `afc keys refresh <key-id> --api-key <key> --hub-url https://hub.example.com`
sends a refresh-key HTTP request to the hub for the specified key_id
generates a new secret for the key and returns the updated key object with the new plaintext secret
prints the updated key object including the new plaintext secret to stdout and exits with code 0
runs `afc keys revoke <key-id> --api-key <key> --hub-url https://hub.example.com`
sends a revoke-key HTTP request to the hub for the specified key_id
permanently marks the key as revoked and returns success
prints a confirmation message to stderr and exits with code 0

execution_paths.title: Login fails due to OAuth callback timeout

steps.action: runs `afc login --provider github --hub-url https://hub.example.com`
fetches and validates the provider list, starts the callback server, opens the browser
does not complete the browser authorization within 5 minutes
callback server times out, shuts down and releases the port
prints a timeout error with a retry suggestion to stderr and exits with a non-zero exit code

error_handling.condition: Hub is unreachable when fetching GET /api/v1/auth/providers during login
error_handling.behavior: Print error to stderr with the attempted hub URL and connection failure details; exit non-zero

error_handling.condition: OAuth callback server does not receive a redirect within 5 minutes
error_handling.behavior: Shut down callback server, print timeout error with retry suggestion to stderr; exit non-zero

error_handling.condition: OAuth provider redirects to callback server with an error parameter
error_handling.behavior: Shut down callback server, print provider error description to stderr; exit non-zero

error_handling.condition: Hub returns HTTP error from POST /api/v1/auth/callback
error_handling.behavior: Parse error envelope, print human-readable message to stderr; exit non-zero

error_handling.condition: Process receives SIGINT/SIGTERM while callback server is waiting for OAuth redirect
error_handling.behavior: Shut down callback server, release port, exit cleanly with non-zero exit code; no orphaned processes or ports

error_handling.condition: Authenticated user is not a member of the specified workspace on afc keys create
error_handling.behavior: Print membership error to stderr; exit non-zero

error_handling.condition: --workspace flag missing on afc keys create
error_handling.behavior: Print usage error indicating required flag is missing to stderr; exit non-zero

error_handling.condition: Neither --api-key nor AF_HUB_API_KEY is provided for a key management command
error_handling.behavior: Print missing credential error to stderr; exit non-zero

error_handling.condition: Hub returns HTTP error on afc keys list
error_handling.behavior: Parse error envelope and print human-readable message to stderr; exit non-zero

error_handling.condition: key_id does not exist or user does not own it on afc keys refresh
error_handling.behavior: Parse hub error envelope, print human-readable error to stderr; exit non-zero

error_handling.condition: No key_id argument provided to afc keys refresh
error_handling.behavior: Print usage error indicating key ID is required to stderr; exit non-zero

error_handling.condition: key_id does not exist or user does not own it on afc keys revoke
error_handling.behavior: Parse hub error envelope, print human-readable error to stderr; exit non-zero

error_handling.condition: No key_id argument provided to afc keys revoke
error_handling.behavior: Print usage error indicating key ID is required to stderr; exit non-zero

error_handling.condition: Hub returns non-2xx response with unparseable error body
error_handling.behavior: Fall back to printing raw HTTP status code and response body to stderr; exit non-zero

error_handling.condition: Neither --hub-url flag nor AF_HUB_URL env var is set for a command that requires the hub URL
error_handling.behavior: Print descriptive configuration error to stderr; exit non-zero

symbols.name: Command
symbols.signature: type Command struct { Use string; Short string; Long string; RunE func(cmd *Command, args []string) error; ... }
symbols.notes: Core Cobra command struct. Signature unverified — cross-check against installed version before implementation.

symbols.name: Command.PersistentFlags
symbols.notes: Returns the persistent flag set that is inherited by all subcommands. Unverified.

symbols.name: Command.Execute
symbols.notes: Entry point to run the CLI. Unverified.

symbols.name: http.Get
symbols.notes: Used to fetch provider list from hub.

symbols.name: http.Post
symbols.signature: func Post(url, contentType string, body io.Reader) (resp *Response, err error)
symbols.notes: Used to send authorization code to hub callback endpoint.

symbols.name: http.Server
symbols.signature: type Server struct { Addr string; Handler Handler; ... }
symbols.notes: Used to implement the local OAuth callback server.

symbols.name: http.ListenAndServe
symbols.notes: Starts the local callback server on the configured address.

symbols.name: exec.Command
symbols.notes: Used to invoke 'open' (macOS) or 'xdg-open' (Linux) to open the authorization URL in the default browser.

symbols.name: Cmd.Start
symbols.notes: Starts the browser-open command without waiting for it to return.
